trans-unit.source: Spatial Processing
trans-unit.target: Spatial Processing

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Buffer
trans-unit.target: Buffer

trans-unit.source: Calculate a ![](/fig/mAlgorithmBuffer.png) `buffer` with a defined distance.
trans-unit.target: Calculate a ![](/fig/mAlgorithmBuffer.png) `buffer` with a defined distance.

trans-unit.source: Dissolve: if 2 or more buffer areas overlap, they can be combined.
trans-unit.target: Dissolve: if 2 or more buffer areas overlap, they can be combined.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Which regions fall within a 100-kilometer radius of major cities? :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Which regions fall within a 100-kilometer radius of major cities? :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Clip
trans-unit.target: Clip

trans-unit.source: With the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool you can extract and retain the spatial extent of one vector layer based on the boundaries of another layer.
trans-unit.target: With the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool you can extract and retain the spatial extent of one vector layer based on the boundaries of another layer.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: refers to the specific __layer to be clipped__, for instance, a road network.
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: refers to the specific __layer to be clipped__, for instance, a road network.

trans-unit.source: `Overlay Layer`: e.g. a polygon layer of the region (e.g. borders of Heidelberg).
trans-unit.target: `Overlay Layer`: e.g. a polygon layer of the region (e.g. borders of Heidelberg).

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Extract the rail network of Germany :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Extract the rail network of Germany :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Dissolve
trans-unit.target: Dissolve

trans-unit.source: The ![](/fig/mAlgorithmDissolve.png) `dissolve` tool aggregates geometries with the same attribute values.
trans-unit.target: The ![](/fig/mAlgorithmDissolve.png) `dissolve` tool aggregates geometries with the same attribute values.

trans-unit.source: When 2 or more buffer areas overlap, they can be combined using dissolve.
trans-unit.target: When 2 or more buffer areas overlap, they can be combined using dissolve.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Extract the rail network of Germany :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Extract the rail network of Germany :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Intersection
trans-unit.target: Intersection

trans-unit.source: The ![](/fig/intersection_icon.png) `intersection` tool extracts the part of the layers which overlap.
trans-unit.target: The ![](/fig/intersection_icon.png) `intersection` tool extracts the part of the layers which overlap.

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Vector` → `Geoprocessing Tool` → `Intersection` OR `Toolbox` → Search for `Intersection`.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Vector` → `Geoprocessing Tool` → `Intersection` OR `Toolbox` → Search for `Intersection`.

trans-unit.source: `Input layer`: select layer one
trans-unit.target: `Input layer`: select layer one

trans-unit.source: `Overlay layer`: select layer two
trans-unit.target: `Overlay layer`: select layer two

trans-unit.source: `Intersection`: Specify where you want to save the results and give it a good name
trans-unit.target: `Intersection`: Specify where you want to save the results and give it a good name

trans-unit.source: Order of input layer and overlay layer does not matter here
trans-unit.target: Order of input layer and overlay layer does not matter here

trans-unit.source: Possibility to keep only certain fields of the output layer
trans-unit.target: Possibility to keep only certain fields of the output layer

trans-unit.source: ⚠️ Attention: Attribute values that refer to output areas (e.g. population) are no longer meaningful after the intersect :::
trans-unit.target: ⚠️ Attention: Attribute values that refer to output areas (e.g. population) are no longer meaningful after the intersect :::

trans-unit.source: `Intersection` operation between a two-features input layer and a single feature overlay layer (left) - resulting features are moved for clarity (right). Source: GISGeography.com :::
trans-unit.target: `Intersection` operation between a two-features input layer and a single feature overlay layer (left) - resulting features are moved for clarity (right). Source: GISGeography.com :::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Intersect countries with timezones
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Intersect countries with timezones
:::

trans-unit.source: Centroids
trans-unit.target: Centroids

trans-unit.source: With the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_centroids.png) `Centroids`-tool, you can create a new layer with points at the centre of each polygon.
trans-unit.target: With the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_centroids.png) `Centroids`-tool, you can create a new layer with points at the centre of each polygon.

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Vector` → `Geometry Tools` → `Centroids`. Alternatively, search for `Centroids` in the [Processing Toolbox]. Open the tool by <kbd>Double-clicking</kbd> on it.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Vector` → `Geometry Tools` → `Centroids`. Alternatively, search for `Centroids` in the [Processing Toolbox]. Open the tool by <kbd>Double-clicking</kbd> on it.

trans-unit.source: `Input layer`: select the polygon layer
trans-unit.target: `Input layer`: select the polygon layer

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: The new layer will be added to your project.
trans-unit.target: The new layer will be added to your project.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Create a centroid for each district in madagascar
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Create a centroid for each district in madagascar
:::
